steps: Go to https://mastodon.social and log in to your account (or create one)
Fill in the 'Application name' field with your app's name
Set the 'Redirect URI' to your OAuth callback URL (use 'urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob' for out-of-band testing)
Select the required scopes: read, write, follow, push
On the application detail page, copy the 'Client key' (Client ID) and 'Client secret'
Use these credentials with the OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow at https://mastodon.social/oauth/authorize

credential_fields.name: client_id
credential_fields.label: Client ID (Client Key)
credential_fields.description: Your Mastodon application Client ID, also called the Client Key

notes: Mastodon is a decentralized network — the base URL depends on the instance (mastodon.social is the flagship)
Each Mastodon instance has its own OAuth app registration at <instance>/settings/applications
Apps registered on one instance cannot be used on another — each instance is separate
The 'urn:ietf:wg:oauth:2.0:oob' redirect URI displays the token on-screen instead of redirecting
Mastodon is free and open source — mastodon.social accounts are free to create
Rate limits apply: typically 300 requests per 5 minutes for authenticated users

test_endpoint.description: Verify the OAuth access token by fetching the authenticated account's profile
